vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The registry-defined specimens (P1-P4) are largely absent or non-viable. The white rabbit anchor is missing from the current frame.

inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss (Only a necrotic leaf remnant remains)

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot; no biological activity detected.
plant_audit.P2: Empty black pot; no biological activity detected.
plant_audit.P3: Contains only a single, shriveled, necrotic leaf fragment; the white rabbit anchor has been removed.
plant_audit.P4: Merged with P2/P3 space; no viable plant matter.

biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshell fragments scattered across the soil surface of the black pot, acting as a non-biological substrate.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_5_to_T_minus_2: Gradual loss of turgor pressure in the P3 specimen; progressive yellowing and chlorosis.
temporal_deltas.T_minus_1_to_Current: Total structural collapse of the P3 specimen; removal of the scale anchor (rabbit); transition to a state of complete biological failure.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome has suffered a total collapse. The lack of light, potential substrate exhaustion, and the presence of debris (eggshells) suggest an environment no longer capable of supporting the registered flora. The plants are effectively dead.
vision_report.anomalies: The introduction of crushed eggshells as a soil amendment/top-dressing is a notable intervention, though it has failed to prevent the systemic loss of the specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical collection. Starting from the earliest image, the P3 specimen showed signs of terminal decline. Over the 5-day observation period, the plant lost all structural integrity, eventually leaving only a necrotic remnant. The removal of the rabbit anchor suggests a cessation of maintenance or a deliberate clearing of the workspace. The current state is one of total botanical vacancy.
